directions.observed: Frontal face with raised muzzle and exposed eyes reads clearly upward.
directions.horizontalEvidence: Not required for the up cardinal.
directions.verticalEvidence: Raised muzzle, eye line, and exposed face point toward the top edge.
directions.reason: Labeled review and blind cardinal majority both confirm up.

directions.observed: Muzzle and eye line shift slightly screen-right while remaining raised.
directions.horizontalEvidence: Nose tip and facial plane move to the screen-right side of head center.
directions.verticalEvidence: Raised muzzle and eyes retain a clear upward pitch.
directions.reason: First coherent up-right step; blind axes pass.

directions.observed: Clear three-quarter screen-right head turn with an upward facial angle.
directions.horizontalEvidence: Snout and facial plane project screen-right.
directions.verticalEvidence: Muzzle and eye line remain visibly raised.
directions.reason: Correct quadrant and smooth progression; blind axes pass.

directions.observed: Strong screen-right turn with a subtle remaining upward cue.
directions.horizontalEvidence: Snout, mouth, and facial plane clearly point screen-right.
directions.verticalEvidence: The upward cue is subtle at normal size but remains visible in the ordered labeled loop.
directions.reason: Blind vertical majority is ambiguous; labeled review confirms up-right with no reversal.

directions.observed: Snout, mouth, and facial plane point unmistakably screen-right.
directions.horizontalEvidence: Nose tip, teeth, and face project toward the right edge.
directions.verticalEvidence: Not required for the right cardinal.
directions.reason: Labeled review and blind cardinal majority both confirm screen-right.

directions.observed: Head remains screen-right as the brim and muzzle begin lowering.
directions.horizontalEvidence: Facial plane and muzzle stay on the screen-right side.
directions.verticalEvidence: Lowered brim and muzzle provide a subtle downward cue in loop context.
directions.reason: Blind vertical majority is ambiguous; labeled review confirms down-right without reversal.

directions.observed: Lowered head and screen-right facial plane read clearly down-right.
directions.horizontalEvidence: Snout and facial plane project screen-right.
directions.verticalEvidence: Bowed head and lowered muzzle read down.

directions.observed: Deep bowed three-quarter head reads down-right over the frontal body.
directions.horizontalEvidence: Subtle screen-right bias remains in the nose and face.
directions.verticalEvidence: Strong bowed-head and lowered-muzzle cue reads down.
directions.reason: Blind axes pass and the boundary to 180 is smooth.

directions.observed: Frontal bowed head, lowered muzzle, and cap brim occluding the eyes read unmistakably down.
directions.horizontalEvidence: Not required for the down cardinal.
directions.verticalEvidence: Bowed face and prominent brim point toward the bottom edge.
directions.reason: Labeled review and blind cardinal majority both confirm down.

directions.observed: Lowered muzzle begins moving screen-left while the head remains bowed.
directions.horizontalEvidence: Nose and facial plane shift to screen-left.
directions.verticalEvidence: Head remains bowed with a lowered muzzle.
directions.reason: Clear first down-left step; blind axes pass.

directions.observed: Three-quarter screen-left facial plane retains a visible downward pitch.
directions.horizontalEvidence: Snout and facial plane clearly project screen-left.
directions.verticalEvidence: Labeled normal-size review shows a lowered muzzle and bowed angle.
directions.reason: Blind vertical majority conflicts, but labeled ordered-loop review confirms down-left with no reversal.

directions.observed: Strong screen-left turn keeps a subtle lowered muzzle and brim angle.
directions.horizontalEvidence: Snout and mouth unmistakably point screen-left.
directions.verticalEvidence: Downward cue is subtle but visible in brim and muzzle angle before 270.
directions.reason: Blind vertical majority is ambiguous; labeled loop confirms down-left without reversal.

directions.observed: Snout, mouth, and facial plane point unmistakably screen-left.
directions.horizontalEvidence: Nose tip and face project toward the left edge.
directions.verticalEvidence: Not required for the left cardinal.
directions.reason: Labeled review and blind cardinal majority both confirm screen-left.

directions.observed: Screen-left facial plane begins lifting through the muzzle and eye line.
directions.verticalEvidence: Muzzle and eye line visibly begin lifting.
directions.reason: Clear first up-left step; blind axes pass.

directions.observed: Raised three-quarter screen-left head reads clearly up-left.
directions.horizontalEvidence: Nose and facial plane clearly project screen-left.
directions.verticalEvidence: Raised muzzle and eye line read up in labeled loop context.
directions.reason: Blind vertical majority is ambiguous; labeled review confirms up-left with no reversal.

directions.observed: Near-frontal raised muzzle retains a slight screen-left bias.
directions.horizontalEvidence: Subtle left bias remains in the nose and facial plane.
directions.verticalEvidence: Raised muzzle and exposed face clearly point up.
directions.reason: Blind axes pass and the boundary to 000 is smooth.
